matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes middle school mathematics students cube and cuboid geometry cylinder surface area & volume cone surface area & volume sphere and hemisphere calculations surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic mathematics: surface areas & volumes what we will learn today learning objectives identify properties of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, spheres, and hemispheres. recall formulas for total surface area (tsa) and volume. apply these formulas to solve numerical problems correctly. the cube: tsa & volume cube geometry side length = 'a' total surface area (tsa) = 6 × a² volume = a³ the cuboid: tsa & volume cuboid geometry dimensions: l (length), b (breadth), h (height) tsa = 2(lb + bh + hl) cm² volume = l × b × h the cylinder: tsa & volume cylinder geometry radius = r, height = h curved surface area (csa) = 2πrh total surface area (tsa) = 2πr(r + h) volume = πr²h the cone: tsa & volume cone geometry radius = r, height = h, slant height = l l² = r² + h² (pythagoras) csa = πrl tsa = πr(r + l) volume = 1/3πr²h spheres & hemispheres: tsa & volume sphere and hemisphere geometry [object object] tsa = 4πr², volume = 4/3πr³ hemisphere...
